ddls:
CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the workclasses table */,
  "fnlwgt" INTEGER /* Final weight, indicating the number of people the entry represents */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the educations table */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Numeric code for the education level */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the marital_statuses table */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the occupations table */,
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the relationships table */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the races table */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the genders table */,
  "capital_gain" INTEGER /* Capital gains of the individual */,
  "capital_loss" INTEGER /* Capital losses of the individual */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Hours worked per week by the individual */,
  "native_country_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the countries table */,
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the incomes table */,
  "disability_status" TEXT /* Disability status of the individual (e.g., yes, no) */,
  "veteran_status" TEXT /* Veteran status of the individual (e.g., yes, no) */,
  "immigrant_status" TEXT /* Immigrant status of the individual (e.g., yes, no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES educations ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_veteran_status FOREIGN KEY ("veteran_status") REFERENCES veteran_statuses ("veteran_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_income_id FOREIGN KEY ("income_id") REFERENCES incomes ("income_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_disability_status FOREIGN KEY ("disability_status") REFERENCES disability_statuses ("disability_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_statuses ("marital_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_relationship_id FOREIGN KEY ("relationship_id") REFERENCES relationships ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_native_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("native_country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_immigrant_status FOREIGN KEY ("immigrant_status") REFERENCES immigrant_statuses ("immigrant_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_workclass_id FOREIGN KEY ("workclass_id") REFERENCES workclasses ("workclass_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workclass */,
  "workclass_name" TEXT /* Name of the workclass */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry related to the workclass */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "educations" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Numeric code for the education level */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study for the education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "job_category" TEXT /* Job category for the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship status */,
  "relationship_name" TEXT /* Name of the relationship status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the race */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incomes" (
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income level */,
  "income_name" TEXT /* Name of the income level */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range for the level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator record */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual */,
  "capital_gain" INTEGER /* Capital gains of the individual */,
  "capital_loss" INTEGER /* Capital losses of the individual */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Hours worked per week by the individual */,
  "unemployment_rate" TEXT /* Unemployment rate at the time of data collection */,
  "inflation_rate" TEXT /* Inflation rate at the time of data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., admin, read-only) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department where the user belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disability_statuses" (
  "disability_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disability status */,
  "disability_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the disability status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disability_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "veteran_statuses" (
  "veteran_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each veteran status */,
  "veteran_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the veteran status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("veteran_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "immigrant_statuses" (
  "immigrant_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each immigrant status */,
  "immigrant_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the immigrant status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("immigrant_status_id")
)
